name: Generative Students Are Evaluated with Profile Alignment and Real-response Item Signals
statement: Lu and Wang evaluate GPT-4-simulated students for question item evaluation through logical and believable response inspection, profile-response alignment, correlations with real student responses, and overlap in difficult questions.
population_or_context: Cambridge International AS and A Level Psychology multiple-choice question item evaluation.
time_horizon: arXiv page submitted 2024-05-19, revised 2024-05-24, retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The arXiv abstract reports 45 GPT-4-simulated student profiles answering 20 multiple-choice questions.
caveats: The staged pass records the abstract and evaluation-summary signals, not the exact numeric correlation table.
Multiple-choice item evaluation differs from constructed-response scoring.
evaluation_method: Profile-response alignment, qualitative believability checks, correlation with real student responses, and overlap in difficult items.
reported_metrics: The source reports correlations with real student responses and overlap in difficult questions; exact numeric values are not extracted in this staged baseline.
limitations: Profile-aligned multiple-choice responses are preliminary item-evaluation support, not substitutes for real student testing.
The staged pass does not extract full numeric results.
The response format and domain are narrow.

source_locator.section: arXiv HTML method and evaluation sections
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the source's profile-simulation setup, response-evaluation summary, real-response correlation, and difficult-question overlap claims.
